findings.title: Significant Relevance Drop for Anonymous Users
findings.description: The new ranking algorithm has caused a 15% drop in search relevance scores, disproportionately affecting anonymous users who make up approximately 40% of queries. This segment was not adequately represented in the pre-launch A/B testing, leading to a significant degradation in their search experience.
assumptions: Anonymous users rely on the search engine without historical session data.

evidence.excerpt: Search relevance score dropped 15% after deploy v2.4.1.
The incident disproportionately affected anonymous users (~40% of query volume).
Results were inconclusive: relevance improved +2% for signed-in users but degraded -8% for anonymous users.

recommendations: Re-run A/B test specifically targeting anonymous users.

findings.title: Increased Mobile Search Latency
findings.description: Post-deployment, mobile search latency increased significantly to 890ms, which is a 112% rise from the baseline of 420ms. This degradation is causing frustration among users, particularly those accessing the service via mobile devices.
assumptions: Users expect fast search results, especially on mobile devices.

evidence.excerpt: P95 search latency on mobile web has climbed to 890ms, up from a baseline of ~420ms.
Support ticket volume increased 3x... complaints fall into two categories: irrelevant search results and slow search on mobile.
Mobile search is really slow. Desktop is fine.

recommendations: Profile the new ranking model for mobile sessions to identify optimization opportunities.

findings.title: Stale Autocomplete Suggestions
findings.description: The autocomplete feature is providing stale suggestions due to a misconfigured cache TTL, leading to user frustration and irrelevant results. This issue compounds the overall relevance problem, as users are directed to zero-result pages.
assumptions: Users rely on autocomplete for quick access to relevant search results.

evidence.excerpt: Autocomplete suggestions are showing stale data.
A smaller but growing segment reports broken autocomplete suggestions leading to dead-end pages.
I keep getting autocomplete suggestions for sales that ended months ago.

recommendations: Correct the autocomplete cache TTL and ensure proper validation of cache configurations.

findings.title: Lack of Instrumentation for New Search Features
findings.description: The new image and voice search features were launched without proper analytics instrumentation, resulting in a lack of visibility into their performance and user adoption. This gap hinders the ability to assess their impact on overall search relevance.
assumptions: Instrumentation is critical for measuring the success of new features.

evidence.excerpt: Missing search analytics events for new query types.
This means we have zero visibility into adoption, success rates, or failure modes for these surfaces.

summary: The analysis reveals critical issues following the v2.4.1 deployment, including a significant drop in search relevance for anonymous users, increased mobile search latency, stale autocomplete suggestions, and a lack of instrumentation for new search features. These findings highlight the need for targeted A/B testing, performance optimization, and improved analytics to restore user satisfaction and search effectiveness.
